Metadata.description: Web UI for MediaMTX. Watch streams, browse recordings, and edit config from your browser.

toolbar.summary: {count, plural, one {# stream} other {# streams}} · {live} playing

errors.unreachableLead: No response from the MediaMTX API at

empty.noStreamsLead: Point any publisher at MediaMTX and it will show up here:

banner.playbackUrlLead: Set the browser-facing MediaMTX URL in App Config to enable live playback.

card.noSnapshot: no snapshot yet — first capture in ~30s

stub.title: Not implemented yet
stub.description: This action is on the roadmap — it was logged.

recordError.title: Couldn't change recording
recordError.description: MediaMTX rejected the change — this stream's recording is unchanged.

copyPublish.title: Publish URLs copied
copyPublish.description: Paste them into your camera or encoder.

snapshotSuccess.title: Snapshot captured
snapshotSuccess.description: The thumbnail now shows the frame you just grabbed.

snapshotError.title: Couldn't capture snapshot
snapshotError.description: MediaMTX couldn't grab a frame — the stream may be offline.

toolbar.summary: {streams, plural, one {# stream} other {# streams}} · {recordings, plural, one {# recording} other {# recordings}}

empty.noRecordingsLead: Recordings appear here once MediaMTX starts recording streams.

errors.readErrorDescription: Make sure the recordings directory exists and has the correct permissions.

appConfig.pageSubHeader: Connection and storage used by MediaMTX Connect.

mediamtxConfig.pageSubHeader: Global MediaMTX server settings, saved to the server.

pathDefaults.pageSubHeader: Settings inherited by every stream, saved to the server.

pathConfig.pageSubHeader: Settings for this stream, saved to the server.
pathConfig.inheritedSubHeader: Settings for this stream, currently inherited from {confName}. Saving overrides only what you changed.

saveBar.unsaved: {count, plural, one {# unsaved change} other {# unsaved changes}}
saveBar.needsAttention: {count, plural, one {# field needs attention} other {# fields need attention}}

mediaMtxUrl.label: MediaMTX URL
mediaMtxUrl.description: The address of your MediaMTX instance. Within a Docker network you can use the container name; otherwise use the external IP or hostname.

mediaMtxApiPort.label: API port
mediaMtxApiPort.description: The port of the MediaMTX API.

remoteMediaMtxUrl.label: Playback URL
remoteMediaMtxUrl.description: The browser-accessible, externally-facing URL of your MediaMTX server. Live playback loads streams from here.

recordingsDirectory.label: Recordings directory
recordingsDirectory.description: Directory containing MediaMTX recordings (not recommended to change when using Docker).

screenshotsDirectory.label: Screenshots directory
screenshotsDirectory.description: Directory for generated snapshots (not recommended to change when using Docker).

toasts.errorDescription: Double-check your form values and try again.

sectionDescriptions.logging: Log output, timeouts and global limits.
sectionDescriptions.api: The HTTP API that MediaMTX Connect itself uses.
sectionDescriptions.hooks: Commands MediaMTX runs on connection events.
sectionDescriptions.rtsp: Publish and read streams over RTSP.
sectionDescriptions.rtmp: Publish and read streams over RTMP.
sectionDescriptions.hls: Serve streams as HLS for browser playback.
sectionDescriptions.srt: Publish and read streams over SRT.
sectionDescriptions.pathHooks: Commands MediaMTX runs on a path's own lifecycle events.

mediamtxForm.hiddenWhileOff: {count, plural, one {# option hidden while off} other {# options hidden while off}}

help.logLevel: Verbosity of the log output (error, warn, info or debug).
help.logDestinations: Where to write logs (stdout, file or syslog).
help.logFile: Path of the log file when logDestinations includes file.
help.writeQueueSize: Size of the outgoing queue per connection. Must be a power of two.
help.udpMaxPayloadSize: Maximum size of outgoing UDP packets.
help.externalAuthenticationURL: URL of an external authentication server, called on every connection.
help.apiAddress: Listen address of the HTTP API.
help.metricsAddress: Listen address of the metrics endpoint.
help.pprof: Expose the Go pprof profiling endpoint.
help.pprofAddress: Listen address of the pprof endpoint.
help.runOnConnect: Command to run when a client connects.
help.runOnConnectRestart: Restart the command if it exits.
help.runOnDisconnect: Command to run when a client disconnects.
help.rtspAddress: Listen address of the RTSP server (TCP).
help.rtspsAddress: Listen address of the RTSPS (TLS) server.
help.rtpAddress: Listen address for RTP packets over UDP.
help.rtcpAddress: Listen address for RTCP packets over UDP.
help.multicastIPRange: IP range used for multicast delivery.
help.protocols: Transport protocols accepted by the RTSP server (udp, multicast or tcp).
help.encryption: TLS encryption policy (no, strict or optional).
help.serverKey: Path of the TLS server key.
help.serverCert: Path of the TLS server certificate.
help.authMethods: Authentication methods accepted by the RTSP server (basic or digest).
help.rtmpAddress: Listen address of the RTMP server.
help.rtmpEncryption: TLS encryption policy for RTMP (no, strict or optional).
help.rtmpsAddress: Listen address of the RTMPS (TLS) server.
help.rtmpServerKey: Path of the RTMPS server key.
help.rtmpServerCert: Path of the RTMPS server certificate.
help.hlsAddress: Listen address of the HLS server.
help.hlsServerKey: Path of the HLS server key.
help.hlsServerCert: Path of the HLS server certificate.
help.hlsAlwaysRemux: Remux streams even when no one is watching.
help.hlsSegmentCount: Number of segments kept in the playlist.
help.hlsPartDuration: Duration of each part in low-latency mode.
help.hlsTrustedProxies: IPs or networks of proxies placed before the HLS server.
help.hlsDirectory: Write segments to disk in this directory instead of RAM.
help.webrtcAddress: Listen address of the WebRTC HTTP server.
help.webrtcServerKey: Path of the WebRTC server key.
help.webrtcServerCert: Path of the WebRTC server certificate.
help.webrtcTrustedProxies: IPs or networks of proxies placed before the WebRTC server.
help.webrtcLocalUDPAddress: Local UDP address used for WebRTC traffic.
help.webrtcLocalTCPAddress: Local TCP address used for WebRTC traffic.
help.webrtcIPsFromInterfaces: Gather candidate IPs from the host's network interfaces.
help.webrtcIPsFromInterfacesList: Interfaces to gather candidate IPs from. Empty means all.
help.webrtcAdditionalHosts: Additional hosts or IPs advertised as WebRTC candidates.
help.webrtcICEServers2: STUN or TURN servers used to establish WebRTC connections.
help.srtAddress: Listen address of the SRT server.
help.recordPath: Path of recording segments. %path is the stream name.
help.recordFormat: Container of recording segments (fmp4 or mpegts).
help.recordPartDuration: Duration of each part in fmp4 recordings.
help.recordDeleteAfter: Delete segments after this duration. Zero disables deletion.
help.runOnInit: Command to run when the path is initialized.
help.runOnInitRestart: Restart the command if it exits.
help.runOnDemand: Command to run when a client starts reading and the path has no publisher.
help.runOnDemandRestart: Restart the command if it exits.
help.runOnDemandStartTimeout: How long to wait for the on-demand publisher to start.
help.runOnDemandCloseAfter: Stop the on-demand command this long after the last reader leaves.
help.runOnUnDemand: Command to run when the on-demand command is stopped.
help.runOnReady: Command to run when the stream is ready to be read.
help.runOnReadyRestart: Restart the command if it exits.
help.runOnNotReady: Command to run when the stream is no longer ready.
help.runOnRead: Command to run when a client starts reading this path.
help.runOnReadRestart: Restart the command if it exits.
help.runOnUnread: Command to run when a client stops reading this path.
help.runOnRecordSegmentCreate: Command to run when a recording segment is created.
help.runOnRecordSegmentComplete: Command to run when a recording segment is complete.

toasts.errorDescription: Double-check your form values and try again.
